human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for capital-markets specificity, post-trade control boundaries, source quality, hold-release discipline, recurring-break escalation posture, and internal consistency across manifest, research, evaluation, deployment, and commercialization artifacts.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to institutional post-trade control work such as allocations, matching, affirmations, SSI quality, settlement-readiness review, break handling, and corrective-action escalation.

Authority sources cover SEC settlement-cycle rulemaking and exam guidance, FINRA books-and-records requirements, DTCC workflow references for CTM and ALERT, and ISITC market-practice guidance for SSI and exception handling.

Evaluation scenarios cover incomplete or contradictory evidence, hold-release refusal, recurring incident escalation, control-bypass rejection, terminology handling, adjacent-lane routing, conflicting policy handling, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/capital-markets-and-asset-management/trade-operations-specialist/ so trade-control work stays distinct from settlements authority, compliance signoff, portfolio management, derivatives, and corporate-actions ownership.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated trade-operations specialist for capital-markets and asset-management post-trade control workflows.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
